summary: Excellent soundboard upgrade captures the classic 12/5/71 New York Felt Forum show, highlighted by standout renditions of "Washed My Hands In Muddy Water," "One More Saturday Night," Jack Straw, Sugaree, Tennessee Jed and a definitive Comes A Time.
review: The Miller‑sourced soundboard tape is praised as the definitive version of this historic New York gig, delivering crystal‑clear mix and no noticeable flaws. Fans rave about several highlights—including Washed My Hands In Muddy Water, One More Saturday Night and early performances of Jack Straw, Sugaree, Tennessee Jed and Comes A Time—while noting Keith Godchaux’s emerging presence in the band.

ai_rating.rationale: All eight reviewers are overwhelmingly positive (average 4.38★). Comments focus on both superb recording quality and strong musical performance, with no substantive criticisms.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Multiple reviewers commend Charlie Miller's upgrade as the definitive, crisp soundboard source. No drop‑outs or muddiness are mentioned.

standout_songs: Washed My Hands In Muddy Water
show_quality.setlist_flow: A classic early‑'71 set that showcases the band in transition; the set flows naturally and captures New York fans’ enthusiasm, with several early versions now considered definitive.

band_member_comments.Keys: Keith Godchaux is noted as coming into his own, adding depth to the keyboards during this period.
